summary.intent: Address Codex review findings on PR #686 (ars-ui) by fixing accessibility issues in the clipboard component: adding native `disabled` to the trigger button and properly associating an optional label via `for` attribute.
summary.outcome: Both third-pass Codex threads were resolved: native `disabled` attribute added to the clipboard trigger, label associated via `for=<trigger id>` without reintroducing `aria-labelledby` precedence conflicts. Changes committed and pushed, with updated snapshots and a fourth Codex review pass triggered.

repo: The project uses `cargo insta` for snapshot testing; snapshots must be accepted with `--accept` after intentional rendering changes.
The project uses `cargo xtask spec validate` for spec validation, separate from unit tests.
Codex review is triggered via `@codex review` comment on the PR; status is tracked by polling GitHub reactions (👀 = in progress, 👍 = approved) and unresolved review threads via GraphQL API.

Using `aria-labelledby` on the clipboard trigger conflicts with the existing dynamic `aria-label` — the correct approach is `for=<trigger id>` on the label element to associate it without overriding accessible name computation.
The repo uses nightly Rust for formatting: `cargo +nightly fmt --all`.
Codex review threads are authored by a user matching `codex` (case-insensitive); this is used to filter unresolved threads that need action.

code.finding: Clipboard trigger was missing the native `disabled` boolean attribute and label association. Fixed by setting `HtmlAttr::Disabled` on the trigger and using `for=<trigger id>` on the label element.
Spec updated alongside implementation to reflect disabled trigger behavior and label association; lines ~348–380 and ~442–458 were the relevant sections.
Label snapshot required regeneration after `for` attribute was added to the label element in the clipboard component.

workflow: Polling Codex review status requires checking both GitHub reactions and unresolved GraphQL review threads; reactions alone are insufficient.
After snapshot changes, the `snapshot-reviewed` label on the PR must be re-applied since the snapshot content changed.
Resolving a Codex thread requires both a reply comment (via `gh api -X POST .../comments`) and a GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation.
Third-pass Codex reviews can take ~10 minutes; polling every 60 seconds with a composite check (reactions + thread count) is the correct pattern.
Format check (`--check`) should be run before committing; auto-fix with `cargo +nightly fmt --all` then re-verify.

friction: Third Codex review pass took ~10 minutes with no intermediate feedback, requiring prolonged polling.
`cargo +nightly fmt --all --check` failed initially, requiring a separate auto-format run before the final commit.
The aria-labelledby fix from a prior pass conflicted with the dynamic aria-label, requiring careful evaluation of the new label association finding to avoid reintroducing the bug.
open_items: A fourth Codex review pass was triggered at the end of the session; its outcome (approval or new threads) is not yet known.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label re-application after snapshot changes may be a manual step that could be automated in the workflow.
